resources.string: This button will take you to the user guides.

When you set a passcode for Nahoft, the app automatically enables two-step login. The first step asks for the passcode you set above. The second step asks for your phone\'s lock - your fingerprint, face, PIN, password, or pattern.
The passcode entered is incorrect. Please try again.
The passcode entered is incorrect. You can try logging in again in 60 seconds.
The passcode entered is incorrect. You can try logging in again in 5 minutes.
The passcode entered is incorrect. You can try logging in again in 15 minutes.
The passcode entered is incorrect. This account has been deleted.
Please wait %1$d minutes %2$d seconds before attempting to log in again.
You have to set a lock screen before using this app.
The passcode cannot be a repeated digit.
The passcode must be 6 digits long.
The destruction code field is empty.
Destruction code entries do not match.
You cannot create a destruction code until you create a passcode.
You cannot have a destruction code that is the same as your passcode.
Your destruction code has been saved.

By activating the "Passcode" option, you can define a six-digit passcode that you will need every time you log in to Nahoft. By activating the passcode feature, the "Logout" option will be activated at the bottom of the main screen.

By enabling the "Destruction code" option, you define a six-digit code that will erase all information stored inside the app, including messages and your friend list. \nUse this code in emergencies to prevent unwanted access to your information.

The Logout option only appears if you have set a passcode. Tap it to immediately log out of Nahoft. If you don\'t use Logout, Nahoft will lock itself after a few minutes of inactivity and require your passcode to unlock.

To edit the name or phone number of your friend, follow these steps: 
 Click once on the name of your friend to go to the chat page. 
 Once inside the chat page, click again on the name of your friend. 
 A new page will open where you can edit the name and phone number of your friend.
Click this button to create a new friend.
To add a new friend, tap the plus (+) icon on the home page.

To delete a friend, follow these steps: \n Hold your finger on the name of the desired friend for a few seconds. \n After a few seconds, you will see a message asking if you are sure you want to delete your friend. By selecting the "Yes" option, your friend will be deleted.

%s needs your security code to exchange encrypted messages with you.\n\nNahoft is offline, so nothing is sent automatically. Tap the button below to share your security code with %s using any messaging app, then ask them to send theirs back to you.

To exchange encrypted messages with %s, you need their security code.

When %s sends you their security code you can share it directly to Nahoft from your messaging app, or you can tap the button below to paste it from your clipboard.

 Tap the share button above if you need to share your code again.
To exchange encrypted messages with %s, they need your security code.\n\nTap the button below to share your security code with them using any messaging app.

This status is displayed with a gray circle next to your friend\'s name. It means you have added your friend to your friend list but no security codes have been exchanged yet - you have not sent your code to your friend, and they have not sent theirs to you.
This status is displayed with an orange circle next to your friend\'s name. It means that your friend has sent you their security code and is now waiting for you to send them yours.
This status is displayed with a blue circle next to your friend\'s name. It means security codes have been exchanged between you and your friend.
This status is displayed with a purple circle next to your friend\'s name. It means that you have sent your security code to your friend and are waiting to receive theirs.

This status is displayed with a green and white confirmation icon next to your friend\'s name. It means that a security code exchange has occurred between you and your friend, and you have securely verified through a trusted third party that your friend was the one who actually sent the code, not an imposter.

This status is displayed with a green and white confirmation icon next to your friend\'s name. It means that a security code exchange has occurred between you and your friend, and you have securely verified through a trusted third party that your friend was the one who actually sent the code, not an imposter.\n Once you are confident about this, you can proceed to the chat page. Click on your friend\'s name and select the \"Verified\" option. By selecting this option, your friend\'s status in Nahoft will change from green (Approved) to a green and white icon with a confirmation sign. This status represents the most secure state in Nahoft.
The security code you imported belongs to you, not to your contact. You\'ll need their Nahoft security code.
You can only message a friend once codes have been exchanged.
You already have a friend with this name.
Something went wrong, we were unable to update your friend status.
Are you sure you want to delete %1$s?
Paste your friend\'s security code here
Paste a code or message before tapping Import.
Your clipboard is empty. Copy a code or message first.
Use this button to import encrypted image messages.
Use this button to import encrypted text messages.
Use this button to send encrypted text messages.
Use this button to send encrypted image messages.
Import an image from your device.
The text you are attempting to send has already been encrypted. Would you like to decrypt it instead of sending it?
Something went wrong. We were unable to decode this message.
Something went wrong. We were unable to process your request.
This image has been saved to your phone.
Are you sure you want to delete this message?
Nahoft is not a messenger, but a tool for encrypting messages
Send encrypted messages as text or image

"Nahoft" is not a messaging app. It is a tool for encryption. Nahoft is an offline software for encrypting text messages on Android phones.\nEncrypting your text messages means that even if your message gets intercepted by a third party, that party can not read it. The only person that can read your message is the one with whom you exchanged security codes (also called public keys).

"Nahoft" is not a messaging app. It is a tool for encryption. Nahoft is an offline software for encrypting text messages on Android phones.
Encrypting your text messages means that even if your message gets intercepted by a third party, that party can not read it. The only person that can read your message is the one with whom you exchanged security codes (also called public keys).
Nahoft is offline, which means you do not need to be connected to the internet to encrypt your messages.
Nahoft only encrypts text messages. Currently, it does not provide encrypted voice and video messages.
Nahoft can encrypt text messages as text. It also can encrypt them as a photo (Steganography.) You can send an encrypted text message or a photo containing a message via another channel that allows you to send the file (for example, SMS, MMS, or a messaging app that allows you to send the file unchanged.) So, the other person, after receiving the message, transfers the encrypted text or photo to Nahoft and reads it. 
For the time being, Nahoft can be installed on phones that use the Android operating system.
Nahoft is not a messaging app. It only encrypts the user's text messages. The user can send the encrypted message to another one through one of the messaging apps (in an insecure network or even using an insecure messaging app.) The other person should be using Nahoft as well. Both should have already exchanged the public keys with one another to enter the messages into this software and read the encrypted messages. Nahoft uses public keys that can be shared with others. It also creates a private key hidden in the app to read the sent messages between two users.
Nahoft's goal is to make it possible for users to send secure messages even through insecure messaging apps and networks. It means that if a user does not have access to secure internet or can not use secure messaging apps (in the case of internet censorship, filtering, and total internet shutdown) and only have access to insecure messaging apps and networks inside the country, even in this case, the users can encrypt the text messages and send them in the network. So, even if the message gets intercepted, it will still not be intelligible since it is encrypted. 
Note: Public keys (also the private key) change whenever you delete and reinstall the app, as well as each time you change your phone and reinstall the app. So, each time you change your phone or delete the app and reinstall it, you need to exchange the keys with your friends.
Important: Nahoft is open-source software, and everyone can have access to its code.
Iranian and non-Iranian experts with experience at major tech companies came together to create Nahoft, with support from the United4Iran Organization.
Keep your conversations private and secure with Nahoft, the end-to-end encrypted communication app.
A security layer for sending messages over insecure channels.\nFully offline - Nahoft operates without servers.\nDestruction code - instantly erase your data.

\"Nahoft\" is one of the apps created in the Iran Cubator 2 program under the supervision of the \"United for Iran\" organization. United for Iran is a human rights organization based in Berkeley, California. Its goal is to promote civil liberties in Iran, defend human rights, support civil society, and encourage citizens to participate through technology.

To send an encrypted text, follow these steps:\n1. Exchange security codes with your friend beforehand.\n2. Go to your friend\'s chat page and write your message at the bottom of the page, in the \"type your message\" section…

To send an encrypted text, follow these steps:
1. Exchange security codes with your friend beforehand.
2. Go to your friend's chat page and write your message at the bottom of the page, in the "type your message" section.
3. Press the send button. A popup will appear with the encrypted text.
4. Copy the encrypted text and send it to your friend through any messaging app you prefer.
To decrypt and read an encrypted text, follow these steps:\n1. Make sure you know who sent the message and that you have previously exchanged security codes.\n2. Copy the encrypted text you received…

To decrypt and read an encrypted text, follow these steps:
1. Make sure you know who sent the message and that you have previously exchanged security codes.
2. Copy the encrypted text you received.
3. Go to the chat page of the friend who sent it.
4. Tap the import icon in the top right corner of the page.
5. Paste the text into the window and tap "Import".
6. The decrypted message will appear in the chat.
To send an encrypted photo manually, two conditions must be met:\nFirst: you know who you\'re sending the photo to and have previously exchanged security codes…

To send an encrypted photo manually, two conditions must be met:
First: you know who you're sending the photo to and have previously exchanged security codes.
Second: you send the photo as a file, not from your gallery. For example, Telegram compresses gallery photos, which makes them impossible to decrypt, but Telegram sends files unchanged, so they can still be decrypted.

With both conditions met, go to the chat page of the friend you want to message. Tap the camera icon at the bottom left of the page. Two new icons will appear:
• The download icon hides your message in a photo and saves it to your device, so you can send it later.
• The share icon hides your message in a photo and immediately opens the share sheet to send it through another app.
To decrypt an encrypted photo manually, two conditions must be met:\nFirst: you know who sent the photo and have previously exchanged security codes…

To decrypt an encrypted photo manually, two conditions must be met:
First: you know who sent the photo and have previously exchanged security codes.
Second: the photo was sent to you as a file and saved to your device as a file. Photos that were compressed in transit (for example, photos sent through Telegram's normal photo upload) cannot be decrypted.

With both conditions met, go to the chat page of the friend who sent the photo. Tap the image icon in the top right corner. A picker will open - choose the photo from your device, and the decrypted message will appear in the chat.
Importing to Nahoft through the share option

Like other apps, Nahoft lets you share text or a photo from your gallery or device storage. Choose Nahoft from the list of suggested apps, and you\'ll be taken to your friend list to select who to send to.

To view your friend\'s security code, go to your chat page with that friend. Click on their name and a new page will open where you can see both your friend\'s code and your own. Your code can also be viewed in the settings.

Your security code is what Nahoft calls your public key. This software utilizes the well-known library libsodium and the Asymmetric Encryption with Authentication method for encryption and decryption. Each user has a private key and a public key. When User 1 wants to send a message to User 2, they use their own private key and User 2\'s public key for encryption…

Your security code is what Nahoft calls your public key. This software uses the well-known library libsodium and the Asymmetric Encryption with Authentication method for encryption and decryption. Each user has a private key and a public key. When User 1 wants to send a message to User 2, they use their own private key and User 2's public key for encryption. The generated message has only two possible ways to decrypt:
• Using User 1's private key (the sender) and User 2's public key.
• Using User 2's private key (the recipient) and User 1's public key.
Therefore, in order for your friends to send you a message, they must have your public key. If you make your public key publicly available, it will not pose any security issues. You can view your public key from the settings.
Button for sending the message via radio.
Narrow band, low speed, high noise resistance
Wider band, faster, standard HF text mode
A %1$s session is already active. Stop it before starting a new session.
Nahoft needs microphone permission to listen for messages over radio. Tap the button below to open settings and grant microphone permission.
A receive session is active. Stop receiving before transmitting.
Eden has returned to receive mode
Message will be sent as plaintext
Message will be received as plaintext
Session started with encryption enabled. Stop to change.
Session started without encryption. Stop to change.
Message is too long to transmit via radio. Please shorten your message.
Signal too weak - check radio gain
The 20-minute session limit was reached. You can start a new session to continue listening.
Received %d signal(s) but no complete messages.
No signals were received during this session.

Radio message reception can take 10 minutes or longer.

A notification allows you to:

• See session progress while using other apps
• Keep the session active when your phone locks
• Return to the session if you navigate away

This notification only appears during active radio sessions and contains no message content.
Without notifications, your phone may stop the radio session when the screen locks or you switch apps.\n\nYou can enable notifications later in system settings if needed.
No packets decoded yet.\nListening for signals…
